title: Etsy to sell music gear marketplace Reverb
text: Etsy announced the sale of Reverb, its music gear marketplace, to Creator Partners and Servco in April 2025. The divestiture reflects Etsy's strategic decision to focus on core marketplace profitability and higher-margin businesses. Reverb generated $917.9 million in gross merchandise sales in 2024, down from $942.1 million in 2023, demonstrating underperformance relative to Etsy's expectations at the time of acquisition. Etsy had acquired Reverb for $275 million in cash in 2019. The sale price was not publicly disclosed, but represented a return of capital to Etsy. The decision to divest followed pressure from activist investor Elliott Management, which had urged Etsy to improve profitability and focus on higher-return-on-capital investments. Following the Reverb sale, Etsy announced the $1.2 billion sale of Depop to eBay in February 2026. These consecutive divestitures demonstrate management's commitment to returning to a pure-play marketplace focused on the core Etsy.com business, where it can achieve superior profitability and capital efficiency.
